questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The figure gets turned to the other side by rotating about the main line joining the arrow with the small line segment.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution: The center geometric shape adds on an additional side. All dots change from white to black, and vice versa.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Problem Figures:
questions.solution: From fig. (2) to fig.(1), the straight lines are converted to curved lines (or arcs).
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Each of the following questions consists of two sets of figures. Figures A, B, C and D constitute the problem set while figures $1,2,3,4$ and 5 constitute the answer set. There is a definite relationship between figures A and B. Establish a similar relationship between figures C and D and choose the one figure from answer set as alternative letter. Problem Figures:
questions.solution: From problem figure first to second whole design reverses vertically. Applying the same rule with the problem figure (C), the answer figure (1) is obtained.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: There is a definite relationship between figures A and B . Establish a similar relationship between figure C and D by choosing a suitable figure (4) from the answer figures. Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The figure is replaced by a figure with two sides move and the new figure is shaded. Similar relationship of (C) is thus figure (3).
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The main figure rotates anti-clockwise but the small circles move faster and go one step ahead, adding one circle with each step.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The whole figure rotates clockwise; the circle at the opening and arms of the swastik change alternately and the black spot moves along with it.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The triangles move along the three points anti- clockwise but change direction, the two circles interchanges with each step.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The pattern in the centre changes alternately and one line is added to branches right and left.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The whole pattern changes alternately and the triangle moves to three positions in a pattern and inverts position.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The required figure should have two arrows with arrowheads reversed.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The upper element gets enlarged and becomes the central element. The central element reduces in size and becomes the lower element. The lower element becomes the upper element.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: Triangle moves along three points remaining upright but shaded area is changing sides. Shaded circle moves diagonally, shaded area alternating up and down
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: Movement of the circle is zig-zag, that of the square is anticlockwise and the third figure goes up and down by turns.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution: Triangles turn to circles and vice versa, and white figures turn to black and vice versa.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution: The circle moves to the bottom of the main figure with black/white reversal.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Each of the following questions consists of two sets of figures. Figures A, B, C and D constitute the problem set while figures $1,2,3,4$ and 5 constitute the answer set. There is a definite relationship between figures A and B. Establish a similar relationship between figures C and D and choose the one figure from answer set as alternative letter. Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The two lower arcs of the right hand side design of problem figure (A) turns over vertically, then the whole design of right hand side reverses horizontally and shifts to left hand side in the problem figure (B). The uppermost arc of left hand side design of problem figure (A) reverses vertically and shifts to the right hand side in the Problem Figure (B). Applying the same rule with Problem Figure (C), the answer figure (3) is obtained.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: Hollow circle and rectangle move along the edges anti-clockwise, solid circle also moves anti-clockwise, but the triangle moves clockwise each time reversing its position.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Which circle should replace the question mark ?
questions.solution: Divide the circles into groups of four. In each group, the circle originally on the far left is moving one place to the right.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: Symbols used for representing cap, forehead, eyes, nose, lips descend one position with each step.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: Triangle rotates anti clockwise through $45^{\circ}$. Circle moves alternately at the two ends.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution:
Clearly fig.(A) rotate through $135^{\circ} \mathrm{CW}$ to from fig. (B) Similar relationship will give fig. (4) from fig. (C).

Hence, fig. (4) is the answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Select the correct figure from among five alternatives given below to fit the pattern
questions.solution: The flag moves towards right going up and down and changing direction, the circle moves along three points anticlockwise and the cross along corners, clockwise.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The two partially shaded circles rotate through $180^{\circ}$.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The figure gets divided into eight equal parts .
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The upper part of the figure gets separated along the line and is lost.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Each of the following questions consists of two sets of figures. Figures A, B, C and D constitute the problem set while figures $1,2,3,4$ and 5 constitute the Answer Set. There is a definite relationship between figures A and B. Establish a similar relationship between figures $C$ and $D$ by selecting a suitable figure from the Answer Set that would replace the question mark(?) in fig. (D). Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The upper-left and the lower-right elements rotate $90^{\circ} \mathrm{CW}$ while the upper-right and the lower-left elements rotate $90^{\circ} \mathrm{ACW}$.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The curved pin rotates $90^{\circ} \mathrm{ACW}$ and moves to the adjacent side (of the square boundary) in a CW direction. The curved arrow rotates $90^{\circ} \mathrm{ACW}$ and moves to the adjacent side in a CW direction
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Figures $A$ and $B$ are related in a particular manner. Establish the same relationship between figures C and D by choosing a figure from amongst the five alternatives, which would replace the question mark in fig. (D) Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The contents of the outer main figure rotate through $45^{\circ} \mathrm{CW}$.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Each of the following questions consists of two sets of figures. Figures A, B, C and D constitute the problem set while figures $1,2,3,4$ and 5 constitute the Answer Set. There is a definite relationship between figures A and B. Establish a similar relationship between figures $C$ and $D$ by selecting a suitable figure from the Answer Set that would replace the question mark(?) in fig. (D). Problem Figures:
questions.solution: The inner element gets enlarged, rotates $45^{\circ} \mathrm{CW}$ and becomes the outer element. The outer element reduces in size, gets vertically inverted and becomes the inner element.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
